medications.drug_name: no acute pharmacological cure for the crash
medications.indication_rationale: No medication reverses a post-infectious crash; management is radical rest, hydration and time, plus screening for the not-to-miss endocarditis.
contraindications_in_this_case: No acute drug reverses the crash

medications.indication_rationale: Maintain hydration with minimal exertion; IV fluids if unable to maintain intake.

medications.drug_name: unproven agents (LDN, NAD+, zinc, vitamin C)
medications.indication_rationale: No evidence for acute crash management.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: In-home support/community nursing for hydration and safety, and to arrange low-exertion bloods/echo.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Mainstays of managing a severe post-infectious crash.
No evidence for acute management; not recommended.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Spouse to repeat a FAST if anything changes and to watch for fever/night sweats/breathlessness, unrousability, or laboured breathing, calling 000 if they occur.
Enforce absolute sensory/cognitive rest (dark, quiet, no screens) and low-exertion hydration.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces energy demand and supports recovery.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use a bedpan and assisted turning to minimise exertion; maintain small frequent fluids.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents further energy drain and dehydration.

patient_education_points.point: This is a severe crash of your post-Q-fever fatigue — your body's energy system is overwhelmed after overdoing it. There's no pill that reverses it; rest, quiet, hydration and time are the treatment.

We've checked for stroke and for signs of a heart-valve infection (a known Q fever complication), and those look clear — so a hospital trip, with its light, noise and effort, would likely make the crash worse.
We should still arrange some low-effort blood tests and a heart scan (echo) as an outpatient to be thorough about the Q fever.
Call 000 if his face droops or one side goes weak, his speech slurs, he develops a fever or night sweats with breathlessness, he can't be woken, or his breathing becomes laboured.

follow_up_plan.interval: In-home support today; telehealth GP review within ~1 week; outpatient bloods + echo for chronic Q fever surveillance
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Crash trajectory, hydration, stroke/endocarditis screen, screening results
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: FAST-positive, fever/night sweats/breathlessness, unrousable, or laboured breathing — call 000.

must_include_items: Perform a spouse-assisted FAST and fever/cardiac (endocarditis) screen
Support radical rest and in-home hydration rather than a harmful ED transfer (when screen clear)
Arrange a low-exertion screening-bloods/echo pathway for chronic Q fever endocarditis
acceptable_alternatives: Ambulance/000 immediately if any red-flag feature is present
errors_of_commission: Reflexive ED transfer worsening the crash
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) a stroke + endocarditis screen, (2) supportive radical rest/hydration rather than automatic ED transfer, and (3) a screening/echo pathway with 000 safety-netting. Both harmful over-triage AND missing endocarditis/dehydration are failure modes.
